headlineSummary: OpenAI and Microsoft Copilot read as mixed-to-neutral, while Anthropic and Google Gemini lean negative; both ends of the spread are close ties, and the negative side rests on limited evidence [E5][E7].

ranking.summary: Google Gemini leans negative on limited evidence: one comment says Gemini CLI randomly breaks and spams content, and another questions whether Gemini can actually carry out the practical task end to end, in a close tie with Anthropic [E7][E8].

Anthropic leans negative on limited evidence: Claude is criticized both for not doing the substantive work in an example and for Claude Code regressions and undocumented changes, with a close tie against the other negative providers [E5][E6].

Microsoft Copilot is mixed-to-neutral with limited evidence: one comment treats Copilot as the benchmark for checkpoints, but another mentions a Copilot spam email, leaving the tone slightly split and in a close tie [E2][E9].

OpenAI looks mixed-to-neutral: one comment praises Codex CLI’s performance and seamless UX, while others complain about slower, frustrating behavior and missing checkpoints, so this stays a close tie on limited evidence [E1][E2][E3][E4].

evidence.excerpt: However, I decided to try codex cli after hearing they rebuilt it from the ground up and used rust(instead of JS, not implying Rust==better). It's performance is quite literally insane, its UX is completely seamless.

annotations.rationale: Explicit 'codex cli' praise says its performance is 'quite literally insane' and its UX is 'completely seamless'.

evidence.excerpt: What I really want from Codex is checkpoints ala Copilot. There are a couple of issues [0][1] opened about on GitHub, but it doesn't seem a priority for the team.

annotations.rationale: Explicitly says Codex lacks a desired feature and criticizes the team for not prioritizing it.
Uses Copilot as the positive benchmark for the desired 'checkpoints' feature.

evidence.excerpt: Going back to codex feels like you're waiting a lot longer for it to do the wrong thing anyway, so the feedback cycle is way slower and more frustrating.

annotations.rationale: Explicit 'codex' is described as slow, error-prone, and frustrating to use.

evidence.excerpt: First OpenAI Engineering blog? I'm definitely interested in seeing more and how they handled the rapid growth.

annotations.rationale: Explicit OpenAI mention and interest in 'seeing more' indicates favorable reception to the engineering post.

evidence.excerpt: However even by that metric I don't see how Claude is doing that. Seth is the one researching the suppliers "with the help of" Claude. Seth is presumably the one deciding when to prompt Claude to make decisions about if they should plant in Iowa in how many days.

annotations.rationale: Explicit 'Claude' is criticized as not actually doing the substantive work in the experiment.

evidence.excerpt: Even Claude Code itself seems to experience an unusually high number of regressions or undocumented changes for such a widely used product.

annotations.rationale: Explicit 'Claude Code' is criticized for regressions and undocumented changes.

evidence.excerpt: Gemini cli just randomly breaks and starts spamming content trying to reorient itself after a while.

annotations.rationale: Explicit 'Gemini cli' is described as randomly breaking and spamming content.

evidence.excerpt: Gemini can wax poetic about optimizing fertilizers usage, grid spacing for best cross-pollination, the optimum temperature, timing, watering frequency of growing corn, but can it actually go to Home Depot, purchase corn seeds, ... (long sequence of tasks) ..., nurture it for months until there's corn in my backyard?

annotations.rationale: Explicit 'Gemini' is portrayed as eloquent but unable to carry out the practical end-to-end task.

evidence.excerpt: I did get the Github Copilot spam email today though.

annotations.rationale: Explicit 'Github Copilot' is associated with unwanted spam email, a mild negative on marketing behavior.
